titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the John Willis Photographs of Arizona, Cambodia, New Mexico, and the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation
Willis (John) Photographs of Arizona, Cambodia, New Mexico, and the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation
titlestmt.author: Matthew Daniel Mason

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday June 1, 2022 at 05:41

did.unittitle: Photographs of Arizona, Cambodia, New Mexico, and the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Inkjet prints of photographs by John Willis that document sites, events, and persons in Arizona, New Mexico, Pine Ridge Indian Reservation, chiefly in 2010-2011. The collection also includes composite images related to Phnom Penh, Cambodia, and a group of images from a series, "Deteriorating Media," that documents deteriorating magazines at the home of Leroy Reddest of Lost Dog Creek, Pine Ridge Indian Reservation.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Inkjet prints of photographs by John Willis that document sites, events, and persons in Arizona, New Mexico, Pine Ridge Indian Reservation, chiefly in 2010-2011. The collection also includes composite images related to Phnom Penh, Cambodia, and a group of images from a series, "Deteriorating Media," that documents deteriorating magazines at the home of Leroy Reddest of Lost Dog Creek, Pine Ridge Indian Reservation.
A panoramic photograph of Arizona shows a "wheatpaste" photographic poster mural at a construction site by physician photographer James Edward Thomas, also known as Chip Thomas or "jetsonorama."
Photographs of New Mexico include views at Almagordo, the White Sands Missile Range Museum, and White Sands National Monument.

Photographs of the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation document activities at the Oglala Lakota Nation Wacipi Rodeo Fair held at the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation, August 2011, which include a traditional pow wow parade and dancing, as well as participants and spectators at a basketball tournament, hip hop dancing competition, and rap music contest. Other images of the reservation include views of the gravesite of Red Cloud at the Holy Rosary Mission Church Cemetery, the Lakota Baptist Church, Yellow Bear Dam, parks, and playgrounds, as well as portraits of Lakota and Oglala individuals and groups.
Signed, titled, and dated by the photographer on versos of prints.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from John Willis on the Walter McClintock Memorial Fund, 2011, and gift of John Willis, 2011.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Arizona, 2011. II. Cambodia, 2010-2011. III. Deteriorating Media, 2011. IV. New Mexico, 2010-2011. V. Pine Ridge Indian Reservation, 2011.

bioghist.head: John Willis (born 1957)
bioghist.p: John Willis is a documentary photographer and instructor of photography at Marlboro College in Marlboro, Vermont. His monographs include Recycled Realities , co-authored with Tom Young (Center for American Places, 2002) and Views from the Reservation (Center for American Places, 2010). Willis has photographs in the permanent collections of the Whitney Museum of American Art, the Museum of Fine Arts in Boston, and the Museum of Fine Arts in Houston, among others. He is also a co-founder with Bill Ledger of the In-Sight Photography Project, a photography outreach project to youth in Brattleboro, Vermont.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: John Willis, Photographs of Arizona, Cambodia, New Mexico, and the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: View of a "wheatpaste" photographic poster mural by physician photographer James Edward Thomas, Jr., also known as Chip Thomas or "jetsonorama."

title.part: Jetsonorama, Navajo Graffiti Artist Image on Stalled Construction, Navajo Reservation, Arizona

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Panorama, printed image, 30.7 x 122.7 centimeters, on paper, 53.4 x 142.4 centimeters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composite photographs from sites in Phnom Penh, Cambodia, that combine copy photographs of mugshots and informal portraits of prisoners at Security Prison 21 (formerly the Chao Ponhea Yat High School) and displayed at the Tuol Sleng Genocide Museum on the same site composited with views of artwork from the Royal Palace also located in Phnom Penh. The inkjet prints in this series measure 16.5 x 35.1 centimeters on paper, 22.6 x 41 centimeters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes images from a series, Deteriorating Media , that documents deteriorating magazines at the home of Leroy Reddest of Lost Dog Creek, Pine Ridge Indian Reservation, 2011. The inkjet prints in this series measure 24.8 x 16.5 centimeters on paper, 31.5 x 23.2 centimeters in window mats.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Image title reflects a varient series title in manuscript inscription on verso by Willis.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The inkjet prints in this series measure 28.8 x 43.2 centimeters on paper of various sizes approximately 33 x 48 centimeters except where noted in this guide.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series III is organized into three subseries: Alamogordo, White Sands Missle Range Museum, and White Sands National Monument.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The White Sands Missle Range Museum documents rockets and missles tested at the United States Army military testing area near Alamogordo, New Mexico, known as Alamogordo Bombing and Gunnery Range (1941), White Sands Proving Ground (1945), New Mexico Joint Guided Missile Test Range (1947), and White Sands Missile Range (1960).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The White Sands National Monument preserves a major portion of a gypsum dunefield in the Tularosa Basin, a graben basin within the Chihuahuan Desert of New Mexico.

title.part: Site of the Missile Testing Range

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series IV is organized into six subseries: Oglala All Nation Pow Wow, Parks and Playgrounds, People, Red Cloud Gravesite, Yellow Bear Dam, and Other Sites.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Images related to the Oglala Lakota Nation Wacipi Rodeo Fair held at the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation, August 5-7, 2011. Willis identifies the event as the "Oglala All Nations Pow Wow." The inkjet prints in this series measure 28.8 x 43.2 centimeters on paper of various sizes approximately 33 x 48 centimeters except where noted in this guide.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under four headings: Parade and Dancing, Basketball Competition, Hip Hop Freestyle Dance Competition, and Rap Contest.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Panorama, printed image, 33.4 x 121.6 centimeters, on paper, 54.8 x 141.7 centimeters.

title.part: Oliver Red Cloud with Guest Protectors

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Image of a Chihuahua dog.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes an image titled, The Kiss .

title.part: Davidica Little Spotted Horse, Son, and Baby "D"
Derrick Janis, Disk Jockey for Kili Radio, Musician, Tattoo Artist, Father

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under four headings: Sarah Ghost Bear, Yellow Bull Family, Other Identified Persons, and Unidentified Persons.

title.part: Sarah Ghost Bear, No Flesh Road, Kyle, South Dakota
Sarah Ghost Bear with Her Children, No Flesh Road, Kyle, South Dakota
Wendell, Dolores, and Jennifer Yellow Bull
Yellow Bull Grandchildren Gathering the Horses
Jake Arapaho, the young Yuwipi Medicine Man, Wilbur Morrison, Ceremonial Singer and Inipi Pourer, Wilbur's daughter and her boyfriend
Gerard Baker, Retired Superintendent of Mount Rushmore National Memorial
Charlyn (Charlie) Bull Bear, Rapid City, South Dakota
Family in Rez Car Day After Tree Fell on It

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gravesite of Red Cloud (Lakota: Maȟpíya Lúta) (1822-1909), a leader of the Oglala Lakota, at the Holy Rosary Mission Church Cemetery, also known as Red Cloud Cemtery, at Pine Ridge.

title.part: Genocide Reminder, American Indian Movement Museum in Wounded Knee
Lakota Baptist Church in Pine Ridge
Summer Storm Clouds, Kyle Housing, Pine Ridge Reservation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Panorama, printed image, 31 x 76.5 centmeters, on paper, 52.6 x 93.2 centimeters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Panorama, printed image, 20.2 x 76.2 centmeters, on paper, 37.2 x 92.5 centimeters.
